director_perception_comment: My partner consistently placed the correct basket quickly and kept the rounds moving. They asked good clarifying questions when a description could match multiple options (e.g., lid open vs closed, handle color/thickness, tray vs tote, loop handles vs arched handle), which reduced ambiguity. There were a couple moments of brief confusion in Round 3 (rechecking Basket 5), but they recovered immediately once I clarified. Overall they were capable, communicative, and our coordination got smoother over time.
matcher_perception_comment: Overall my partner gave clear, distinctive visual descriptions (colors, handle types, lids/animal shapes, bands) that were usually enough to identify the right basket quickly. They responded well to clarifying questions (e.g., handle color, lid vs no lid, tray vs tote) and corrected ambiguity when I flagged potential confusions. A few moments in round 3 had some repetition/misalnumbering (e.g., re-stating “pos5” while I was asking for basket 6/8), which briefly slowed things down, but the coordination still improved across rounds and we finished accurately.
